entry.title: Re: Can we have person id set different than User ID via...
entry.content:
Hi,

One thing you can try, if the generation of Person Id has a simple logic, is to use Intelligent Services in combination with Integration Center.

You can use the Employee Hire event in Intelligent Services and then create an integration in Integration Center to update the Person Id field. You must make sure the Person Id is unique.

Thanks,

Takashi

entry.title: Data Modelling @ SAP Sapphire 2026 - Key Sessions to Attend
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is fast approaching, and if you’re a data modeler, this is your opportunity to learn how you can sharpen your skills and expand what’s possible by leveraging a business data fabric. Implementing a business data fabric using  is an important step towards successfully implementing AI. Data modelers are key to making that happen, and this year’s Sapphire sessions will highlight some of the latest and greatest available to data modelers.

Not sure what your agenda looks like? I’ve made a curated list of Sapphire Orlando sessions this year that are relevant to the role of a Data Modeler for you to attend.  

Session Recommendation 1 - Getting to know data products in SAP Business Data Cloud: BDC2747
May 11, 11:30 AM – 11:50 AM EDT 

First up, I’d recommend session BDC2747, which covers data products in SAP Business Data Cloud. For data modelers, this one is foundational. The session breaks down what SAP data products are, how they function within the SAP BDC ecosystem, and why they represent a fundamental shift in how data modelers deliver value.  

What makes this session particularly relevant for modelers is the emphasis on pre-built business semantics. These data products come with built-in context, allowing modelers to move beyond building from scratch and instead focus on extending and composing on top of curated, trusted assets. 

Session Recommendation 2 - SAP BDC Connect: Unlocking SAP context across your data and AI landscape: BDC2547
May 11, 4:00 PM – 4:20 PM EDT 

Session BDC2547, will showcase SAP Business Data Cloud Connect. This capability makes business context available across the clouds and platforms in your ecosystem with zero copy. As a data modeler, this session will show you how to design models that access data from multiple sources, while maintaining their original business context without replication. 

Session Recommendation 3 - Share calculation views and data products across SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BDC: BDC2187
May 12, 2:00 PM – 2:20 PM EDT 

As a data modeler, you know the challenge of building something once and making it available everywhere, without losing semantic integrity. Session BDC2187 speaks directly to this challenge by exploring the interoperability between SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud for sharing trusted assets at scale. For modelers, the focus on governed semantic models and secure data sharing is especially valuable because it validates an approach where your work becomes a reusable building block across analytics, AI, and application scenarios.  

Session Recommendation 4 - Getting started with the data product studio in SAP Business Data Cloud: BDC2743 
May 13, 11:30 AM – 11:50 AM EDT 

Even though it's listed last – this session should be at the top of your list. BDC2743 focuses on the Data Product Studio and how to operationalize data products within your data ecosystem. This session will provide you with practical advice to help you get started building data products in your own environment.  For data modelers, this means new design patterns, evolving metadata considerations, and a more intentional approach to how downstream consumers discover and trust what you build. If you're looking for a clear starting point to translate your modeling expertise into governed, discoverable data products that power analytics and AI, this is it. As a bonus, expect a look at new agentic AI features that further streamline the process. 

Beyond the Sessions 

Block time to visit the SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BDC demo areas on the exhibition floor. Have a focused conversation with a product expert about your specific modeling challenges, or how these emerging tools can help you in your daily activities 

Before You Arrive at Sapphire 

If you haven’t already, explore the SAP Business Data Cloud trial environment and experiment with the Data Product Studio. You’ll get hands-on experience with BDC before the event day, and if you have questions, our experts at the Sapphire booths will be more than happy to support you. Finally, connect with the Data Professionals community group to continue the dialogue well after the event ends.

entry.title: 管理自动付款应用中的附件和注释新功能支持
entry.content:
1. 业务背景

在客户付款流程中，需要根据供应商发票、合同、银行确认或审批备忘录等支持文档来证明和验证付款请求的合理性和真实性。

 如果您正在使用已激活的范围项目 J78 中的工作流功能。目前，管理自动付款应用不支持在付款请求级别上载或处理附件或输入注释的功能。

这会导致：

  • 需要额外的系统外的手动流程（比如通过电子邮件、共享文件夹来分享以上提到的证明文件）
  • 系统外流程导致丢失严密的审计线索链接
  • 审批处理效率低下，需要同时比对系统外原始文档和系统内待审批条目
  • 缺少注释，无法给不知情的审批人解释付款审批的缘由，增加沟通成本
  • 存在合规性和审计风险

云系统时代，客户需要端到端数字付款审批流程，包括提供上载附件、增加注释的功能。

这正是新 SAP CIP “Attachment and Note Support in Manage Automatic Payments”

（在 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/370881/?section=sectionDetails）至关重要。

  1. 当前限制
  • 通过“管理自动付款”创建的付款请求不允许上载附件或增加注释
  • 工作流审批人无法访问支持文档，需要通过系统外辅助方式来实现
  • 附件不得不在 SAP 外部发送（电子邮件、SharePoint 等）
  • 没有链接到付款凭证的集中审计线索
  1. 请求的增强

要求 1：付款请求中的附件上载

允许用户直接在 管理自动付款 应用中上载附件和增加注释。

详细信息：

  • 每个付款申请允许多个附件
  • 支持常见文件类型（PDF、DOCX、XLSX、JPG、PNG等）
  • 使用 SAP 附件服务安全存储的文件（例如GOS/DMS/附件服务）
  • 附件和注释可见于：
    • 付款请求抬头
    • 项目级别（可选）

业务价值：

  • 完全数字付款请求流程
  • 降低运营间接费用
  • 提高审计就绪程度
  • 提高财务和应付账款团队的透明度

要求 2：通过工作流审批的附件流

确保所有上载的附件在审批工作流中自动可用。

详细信息：

  • 附件应为：
    • 已传递到管理付款审批工作流
    • 对 中的所有审批人可见：
      • 我的收件箱
      • 工作流审批屏幕
  • 附件必须为：
    • 可查看
    • 可下载
  • 基于权限控制访问

业务价值：

  • 更快速、更明智地审批
  • 由于缺少信息，减少审批拒绝
  • 加强内部控制与合规性
  • 从请求到审批再到付款，实现全面的可追溯性
  1. 合规与审计优势
  • 链接到付款交易的集中凭证资源库
  • 为内部和外部审计提供清晰的审计跟踪
  • 与 SOX 和内部财务控制要求保持一致
  • 降低运营风险，减少人为错误
  1. 目标成果

通过实施附件支持和工作流传播：

  • 付款审批完全数字化且合规
  • 审批周期时间显著缩短
  • 客户避免自定义开发或外部工具
  • 功能与 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 最佳实践保持一致
  1. 优先级和影响
  • 优先级：高
  • 影响领域：财务运营、应付账款、内部控制
  • 客户类型：使用自动付款的所有 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户，尤其是审批人一般是公司中高级管理人员，包括但不限于采购部经理，财务部经理，资金部经理，财务总监，运营总监，甚至是总裁等。他们都是对公司运营，包括购买云ERP有决策影响的人，让他们有好的业务体验，非常重要。

✅ 此增强将显著提高管理自动付款应用的可用性、控制和企业准备情况。

entry.title: Re: How can I master the digital business transformation to achieve high and sustainable user adopti
entry.content:
I appreciate Margit sharing this learning journey — the people side of SAP Cloud projects is genuinely critical and often underestimated.

That said, I strongly agree with the spirit of many comments here: true digital business transformation isn't mastered through a tool or a single learning journey. It's a continuous, organization-wide discipline.

From my experience as VP of IT leading multiple transformations, sustainable user adoption only happens when:

  • The solution solves real business pain users feel daily
  • Leadership treats adoption as a 12–24 month journey, not a go-live event
  • We remove friction instead of just pushing training & communication
  • We measure business outcomes, not system usage metrics

The SAP OCM learning path is helpful for project-level execution, but real mastery comes from combining strong change management with ruthless process simplification and cultural alignment.

Curious to hear from others — what's the biggest adoption blocker you've faced in your SAP/cloud transformations?

Best regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: Consumption Mode 5 with Planning Period P
entry.content: Pure configuration is not possible for fiscal period PIR consumption in SAP. Consumption mode 5 is limited to M/W periods. Custom BAdI implementation is required to handle fiscal periods and flexible backward/forward offsets.

entry.title: Re: Does pMRP Consider Routing Operation Overlapping and...
entry.content:
Please note pMRP is designed as a strategic simulation tool to evaluate capacity and material availability under different demand scenarios. It deliberately simplifies the lead‑time calculation model compared to classic MRP. As a result, detailed scheduling elements such as operation overlapping maintained in routings and lead‑time offsets maintained in BOM components are not considered in pMRP runs. Instead, pMRP uses basic lead‑time data (procurement type, in‑house production time, planned delivery time, etc.) to simulate material flows and capacity requirements.

If you need simulations that respect detailed scheduling logic (like overlaps and offsets), you would need to rely on classic MRP or detailed scheduling in PP/DS rather than pMRP.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in April
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in April

SAPPHIRE season is upon us 🌴 and the April releases didn't hold back either — plenty of useful features landing across monitoring, task chains, data products, and more.

Learn more in my top features video in April 2026 on YouTube 📺.

In addition, explore the latest updates in our community news blogs and more.
 

 

My top features in April

Update Action for Installed Data Products

Data products installed in an SAP Datasphere space now have an Update action button, available when new minor versions are released. This improvement streamlines the workflow, providing a more efficient and intuitive process for users tasked with keeping data products current.

➡️  More information Installing Data Products and Data Product Details.

 

BDC Cockpit Data Product Monitoring

As promised, the BDC Cockpit is being continuously improved. Administrators now get significantly better visibility into SAP-managed data product pipelines — this is the first step of several improvements coming over the next months.

The cockpit now shows the full pipeline status at every step, making it easy to identify exactly where issues occur and act before they impact your business.

 

Local Table Partitioning

In the table editor, you can create partitions on existing SAP-Managed local tables stored in a standard space (with SAP HANA Database (Disk and In-Memory) storage) that already contain data. So previously unpartitioned tables can now be partitioned even if they already carry data. This is particularly useful for large-volume tables where partitioning was not possible at initial creation.

➡️  More information Partitioning Local Tables and Partitioning Local Tables for Intelligent Applications.

 

Spark Configuration

The Limits to the amount of compute resources that Apache Spark applications can consume when running tasks are set with configurations. In addition to using the provided configurations, which are applied by default to new file spaces, you can now change the default configuration for a task activity in each file space, and create your own configurations.

Be aware, that newly provided default configurations for task activities may allocate fewer resources than those used in previous versions (see SAP Note 3726059), tasks might run more slowly even though they consume less compute. If these new default values don't meet your needs, you can change the default configuration for a task activity in each file space.

➡️  More information.

 

Cloud Connector Support for Google BigQuery

You can now configure private connectivity for Google BigQuery connections through the Cloud Connector. The Cloud Connector routes data traffic through secure tunnels rather than the public internet, allowing you to use replication flows to write data to Google BigQuery target systems that don't have public endpoints.

➡️  More information.

 

Task Chains Deletion Types

Task chains now support all deletion types for both local tables and Local Tables (file).

The newly supported deletion activities are:

  • Delete All Records
  • Delete Filtered Records

➡️  More information.

 

Semantic Onboarding of Calc Views from HANA Cloud

You can import calculation views from SAP HANA Cloud as remote tables while preserving semantic information. This helps you leverage existing HANA Cloud models without recreating them, keeping their business context and metadata for consistent data modeling across platforms.

Be aware that as a prerequisite, only calculation views deployed in SAP HANA Cloud version QRC 1/2026 or higher can be imported in SAP Datasphere database version 2025.50 or higher.

To ensure that your calculation view can be imported, deploy the calculation view in the source system with SAP Business Data Cloud Integration Mode set to Flat View or Construction Kit.

➡️  More information

  • Importing Entities with Semantics from SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP HANA Connections
 

Analytic Model — Inherited and Local Properties

A small but useful addition to the analytic model. For analytic models that use another analytic model as source, you can switch on Show Inherited Elements in the properties panel, to display the inherited elements. That makes it easy to distinguish between local and inherited properties.

➡️  More information.

 

Improved Monitoring Tool Navigation

The Monitoring tool now features an enhanced navigation menu for easier access to key monitoring pages:

  • System and Spaces – Monitor system performance, storage, tasks, and out-of-memory issues across all spaces. Track storage consumption and Apache Spark usage.
  • Capacities Monitoring – Track monthly and daily capacity unit consumption against your subscription and download detailed hourly data.
  • Task Logs – Review task execution history and performance.
  • Expensive Statement Logs – Monitor SQL statements exceeding configured thresholds.
  • Elastic Compute Nodes - Monitor elastic compute nodes key performance figures.
  • Data Integration – Monitor, schedule, and run data replication tasks for remote tables and views. Manage flows and task chains.

That consolidates all the monitoring activities into one menu section.

➡️  More information.

 

 

SAPPHIRE 2026 — Let's Meet in Orlando

SAP SAPPHIRE 2026 is just around the corner! I'll be in Orlando presenting the introductory session on SAP Business Data Cloud — a 20-minute guided tour of what it is, how it's positioned, and where it fits into SAP's data and analytics strategy. I'm running it twice:

📅 Monday, May 11 | 1:30 PM – 1:50 PM EDT

📅 Tuesday, May 12 | 4:00 PM – 4:20 PM EDT

With 30+ sessions on Business Data Cloud at this year's SAPPHIRE, there's a lot to explore. You'll also find us at the SAP Business Data Cloud booth with six demo stations and hands-on sessions. No excuse not to stop by if you're in Orlando.

Can't make it in person? All content will be available online — and of course, I'll keep publishing the monthly newsletter right here.

🔗 Session link | 🔗 My SAPPHIRE blog

 

Webinar Series: The Next Era of Business Data

The last session of the great series has just been streamed on YouTube. In case you could not make it live, all sessions are recorded and I added the links to the recordings below.

entry.title: Re: BAPI/FM/API for KKPAN in SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
To my knowledge, there is no standard BAPI or released API for transaction KKPAN. SAP has not provided a direct function module for mass unit costing; the usual approach is either batch input (BDC), using report programs, or leveraging enhancement frameworks.

Having said that, the recommended path can be

1) For mass unit costing
Build a custom ABAP report that either uses BDC for KKPAN or wraps internal costing FMs. Ensure logging and error handling for materials that fail costing.

2) For long-term stability
Raise a request with SAP if you need a released API. SAP may suggest alternatives like using product cost planning APIs (which exist for CK transactions, but not for KK).

entry.title: Re: Provide a CDS view for Overhead rate in Costing Sheet
entry.content: The CDS view you need is I_OverheadRateInCostingSheet, already released in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. You can build your own analytical query on top of it to provide the month‑end overhead rate report for your customer.

entry.title: Re: Stock transfer price differs in supplying and receivi...
entry.content:
SAP standard does not allow you to simply “change the valuation price” during stock transfer, but there are supported ways to model this

Some of the standard options are

1. Stock Transport Order (STO) with Condition Technique
Create an intercompany STO between supplying plant M121 and the receiving plant. In the STO pricing procedure, maintain a pricing condition (e.g., PB00) at 700 INR (market price). The supplying plant issues delivery and billing; the receiving plant posts GR at 700 INR. This way, the receiving plant inventory is valued at 700 INR, while the supplying plant still carries 1000 INR cost in its books. The difference (300 INR) is posted to intercompany clearing accounts.

2. Transfer Pricing in Controlling
SAP supports multiple valuation approaches (legal, group, profit center). You can configure transfer prices so that Supplying plant uses cost-based valuation (1000 INR). Receiving plant uses market-based valuation (700 INR). This requires activation of parallel valuation in CO and customizing transfer pricing.

3. Special Procurement Key
Define a special procurement key for the receiving plant to source from M121. Combine with STO pricing so that the valuation in the receiving plant reflects the market price.
